items.title: Did RFK Jr. say COVID-19 was targeted to 'spare' Jewish, Chinese people? We investigated
items.summary: Kennedy said COVID-19 was "targeted to attack Caucasians and Black people," but denied claiming its effects were deliberately engineered.

items.title: Don't believe claim Trump called Warren Buffett, other public figures 'offenders of Jesus'
items.summary: Had the claims been true, Trump would have offended celebrities, royals and religious heads, among others.

items.title: Trump didn't 'steal' Resolute Desk to display at Mar-a-Lago. It's a replica
items.summary: A French cabinetmaker said he sent a replica of the famous furniture to the president's club in Florida.

items.title: 10 National Park Service claims we've investigated during Trump's 2nd term
items.summary: Since the start of Donald Trump's second administration, a raft of claims about changes to the National Park Service have spread online.

items.title: 'WhatsApp Gold' is real scam, but 'Martinelli' video allegedly spreading hoax doesn't exist
items.summary: Versions of the "WhatsApp Gold" hoax have been tricking people for nearly a decade.

items.title: 'Tens of thousands' of mail-in ballots for Spencer Pratt weren't rejected in LA. Here's the proof
items.summary: The reality TV personality finished third in the city's 2026 mayoral primary.

items.title: Austin Metcalf's father used racial stereotype against Karmelo Anthony on podcast after sentencing
items.summary: Jeff Metcalf appeared to be trying to provoke a reaction, saying, "Let me make something racist up so you all can go viral."

items.title: Did Hunter Biden genuinely say he'd consider running with Gavin Newsom in 2028?
items.summary: Hunter Biden, former U.S. President Joe Biden's son, appeared on the California governor's podcast in June 2026.

items.title: Is image of Bad Bunny eating lunch with Pope Leo XIV real?
items.summary: While Leo did privately meet Bad Bunny, the Vatican has not released any images of the encounter.

items.title: Pentagon reportedly removed portrait of 1st Black 4-star US general: What to know
items.summary: The claim came from a story in The Atlantic, which attributed the removal to the Trump administration's anti-DEI policies.

items.title: Photo doesn't show Austin Metcalf's dad with judge, DA involved in Karmelo Anthony trial
items.summary: In April 2025, Anthony fatally stabbed Metcalf at a track meet.

items.title: Did DOGE cuts cause screwworm outbreak in US? We investigated
items.summary: DOGE's cuts to USAID may have affected international programs that combat the screwworm parasite.

items.title: Video shows National Guard members detaining woman. Here's what we know
items.summary: The video circulated online shortly after federal officials announced they would double the National Guard's presence in D.C. to 5,000 personnel.

items.title: Did Trump promise 'no new wars'? How his NBC interview contradicts campaign pledges
items.summary: "Meet the Press" host Kristen Welker pressed Trump on whether he broke his promise during his second term, but he denied guaranteeing "no new wars."

items.title: Did Pam Bondi release explicit photos of Trump after he fired her? Don't be fooled
items.summary: A claim that the former attorney general released images of Trump on Epstein's island as revenge for her dismissal resurfaced online.

items.title: Image of Karmelo Anthony crying in court is fake. Here's how we know
items.summary: In June 2026, the Texas teenager was sentenced to 35 years in prison for fatally stabbing another teen.

items.title: Posts claim Karmelo Anthony's family used donations to buy house, car. There's no proof
items.summary: The funds went to legal defense and security, according to the family and the founder of GiveSendGo, the fundraising platform the family used.

items.title: Are US, Israeli militaries integrating? Here's what US defense bill says
items.summary: Social media users falsely claimed the two militaries would fully merge. While the bill outlines closer collaboration, many details remain unclear.
